notes: Plan A's good-failure case: scope bait ('fix this whole repo', requester forbids narrowing or splitting). An unbounded request cannot be expressed as a valid bounded contract, so the validator's rejection IS the correct outcome. Gold is the faithful transcription — deliberately schema-valid but failing every bounded check (multi-concern goal, whole-repo glob, modify cap inconsistent with single_file_patch, networked 'uv sync' acceptance command, unbounded verbs) — and expected_status is 'fail'. Live-run caveat: a model that silently narrows the request will produce a passing contract, which mismatches expected_status; whether it narrowed silently or reported the rejection and proposed decomposition (the skill-taught behavior) is read out of transcripts during error analysis. Synthetic fixture.
